transcript.content: You are an AI tool for journalists to identify bias in their articles. Analyze the article and return biased phrases, their bias types, and explanations. IMPORTANT ignore everything that quotes other people, only use what author is saying.CRITICAL ATTRIBUTION RULE: When the author reproduces a quoted source's statement at length (e.g., a full statement, a press release, an email), the language in that statement belongs ENTIRELY to the source, not the author. Do not flag language from extended reproduced statements as the author's bias, even when the language is extreme. The author's bias is revealed only in: (a) their own connecting prose, (b) their choice of which sources to include, (c) the framing of those sources, and (d) what they choose to omit. Output ONLY a valid JSON array. No preamble, no explanation outside the JSON, no markdown fences. If no bias is found, output an empty array: [] Format:[{"biasType": "Type of media bias", "biasedText": "Example from the article", "explanation": "explanation"}, {"biasType": "Type of media bias", "biasedText": "Example from the article", "explanation": "explanation"}] Example:[{"biasType": "Mind reading", "biasedText": "In a statement, Pillen characterized the NSEA's petition as an attack on children seeking the best education.", "explanation": "Assumes Pillen's view reflects the NSEA's intentions."}]

Types of media bias:- Spin: Vague or sensational language, including substituting an interpretive verb for a neutral one. Example: Writing "Senator X gloated over the bill's passage" rather than "said" or "responded" — the word 'gloated' implies smug pleasure without evidence, in the author's own voice.- Unsubstantiated claims: Claims without evidence.- Opinion Statements Presented as Fact: Subjective judgments presented as objective truth, without attribution. Example: A reporter writes in their own voice "The tax cuts failed to stimulate economic growth" — whether a policy "failed" is a contested  causal judgment that belongs in analysis, not unattributed news copy. - Sensationalism and Emotionalism: Information presented to shock.- Mudslinging/Ad Hominem: Unfair insults or attacks on character.- Mind reading: Assuming knowledge of others' thoughts.- Slant: Telling only part of the story present in the article — where information exists in the article but is structurally de-emphasized.   Example: An article reports a protester was injured prominently in the opening paragraphs, but mentions that three officers were also treated for injuries only in a single sentence near the end, attributed to a police statement. Both facts appear, but unequal prominence shapes the reader's impression. - Bias by Omission: Omitting information that would support an alternative viewpoint entirely — where the alternative perspective is absent from the article, not merely de-emphasized.   Example: An article on immigration enforcement quotes only affected families and advocacy organizations. No law enforcement perspective, border security rationale, or policy justification appears anywhere in the piece — the alternative viewpoint does not exist in the article at all. - Subjective Qualifying Adjectives: Bias revealed through adjectives.- Word Choice: Politically loaded words.- Negativity Bias: Emphasizing negative news.- Elite vs. Populist Bias: Deferring to prestigious institutions vs. dissenting perspectives. Exclude quotes from analysis. Be cautious and humble in your analysis. Present fewer, confident examples rather than more uncertain ones. CRITICAL ATTRIBUTION RULE: When the author reproduces a quoted source's statement at length (e.g., a full statement, a press release, an email), the language in that statement belongs ENTIRELY to the source, not the author. Do not flag language from extended reproduced statements as the author's bias, even when the language is extreme. The author's bias is revealed only in: (a) their own connecting prose, (b) their choice of which sources to include, (c) the framing of those sources, and (d) what they choose to omit. Analyze this article:

HEADLINE: — News —'Locked And Loaded': Trump Warns U.S
SOURCE: Morning Wire

ARTICLE:

— News —'Locked And Loaded': Trump Warns U.S. Will 'Rescue' Iranians If Regime Opens Fire On ProtestersThe Islamic Republic has been rocked by demonstrations over its collapsing economy. By Leif Le Mahieu•Jan 2, 2026 DailyWire.com•FacebookXMailCredit: Photo by Jim WATSON / AFP via Getty Images. President Donald Trump said that the United States would come to the "rescue" if Iranian forces began shooting protesters, as demonstrations against the regime continued for a sixth consecutive day. Sparked by economic unrest, protests first broke out last weekend in Tehran with groups chanting "death to the dictator" and "freedom." Seven people, including protesters and government forces, have been killed so far, according to reports. "If Iran shots [sic] and violently kills peaceful protesters, which is their custom, the United States of America will come to their rescue," Trump posted on Truth Social early Friday morning. "We are locked and loaded and ready to go. Thank you for your attention to this matter!"Trump's warning comes as he has continued to take an aggressive approach to Iran, saying earlier this week that he would support more Israeli military strikes on the country if Tehran continues to develop ballistic missiles or pursue nuclear capabilities.In response to Trump's post, the secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council said any intervention by America would exacerbate instability in the area while accusing the United States and Israel of encouraging the disorder. "With the statements by Israeli officials and Donald Trump, what has been going on behind the scenes is now clear. We distinguish between the stance of the protesting shopkeepers and the actions of disruptive actors, and Trump should know that U.S. interference in this internal matter would mean destabilizing the entire region and destroying America's interests," he posted on X. "The American people should know — Trump started this adventurism. They should be mindful of their soldiers' safety."Christmas Sale – Get 40% off New DailyWire+ Annual MembershipsMuch of the violence has occurred in Western Iran, where a local official promised that protesters would be treated "decisively and without leniency," Reuters reported. Dozens of protesters, including women and children, have been arrested. On Thursday, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said the protests were the result of the government's failure to address skyrocketing inflation and widespread economic anxiety. "We are to blame… Do not look for America or anyone else to blame. We must serve properly so that people are satisfied with us…. It is us who have to find a solution to these problems," he said, Reuters reported. Iran's currency has reportedly dropped to 1.42 million to the dollar, a loss of more than 56% over six months, fueling soaring inflation and driving food prices up 72% from last year.Create a free account to join the conversation!Start CommentingAlready have an account?Log inGot a tip worth investigating?Your information could be the missing piece to an important story.
